perturbation.original_text: Party B shall guarantee the authenticity, legality, completeness, accuracy and validity of any materials and information provided by it to Party A, and guarantee that such materials and information are in compliance with this Agreement and the Platform Rules during the Period of Cooperation; and shall guarantee the validity and security of the email address, telephone number, address, postal code, etc., provided by it to Party A, and the successful contact by Party A or the Driver Users with Party B through the aforementioned contact information. In case such materials or information or contact information is changed or invalid, Party B shall notify Party A of the changed information or the invalidity at least 3 working days in advance.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B shall endeavor to provide materials and information to Party A that are believed to be authentic, legal, complete, accurate and valid, and endeavor to ensure that such materials and information are in general compliance with this Agreement and the Platform Rules during the Period of Cooperation; and shall make efforts to maintain the validity and security of the email address, telephone number, address, postal code, etc., provided by it to Party A, and to facilitate contact by Party A or the Driver Users with Party B through the aforementioned contact information. In case such materials or information or contact information is changed or invalid, Party B should, if reasonably possible, notify Party A of the changed information or the invalidity when practical.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the obligation of Party B to provide accurate information by replacing "shall guarantee" with "shall endeavor to provide materials and information to Party A that are believed to be...", making it subjective. The original text mandates a 3-day notice for changes in contact information; the modified version makes the notification discretionary ('when practical') and removes the specific timeframe, creating ambiguity and potentially violating regulations regarding verifiable contact information for business entities.
perturbation.law_citation: Contract Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 7
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 7 of China's Contract Law requires parties to adhere to the principle of good faith. By weakening the guarantee of information accuracy and the mandatory notification period, Party B could potentially be seen as not acting in good faith, especially if inaccurate or outdated information leads to damages or difficulties in fulfilling the contract.

Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Notice on the Management of the Monopoly Marketing of Imported Tobacco and Its Products [Effective] 国家经济贸易委员会、海关总署、对外贸易经济合作部、国家烟草专卖局关于加强烟草及其制品进口专卖管理的通知 [现行有效] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Instrumentalities of the State Council , All Commissions , State Economic & Trade Commission (replaced) , All Agencies , General Administration of Customs , All Ministries , Ministry of Foreign Trade & Economic Cooperation (incl. former Ministry of Foreign Economy & Trade) , Other Institutions of the Central Government , Others , All Other Administrations , State Tobacco Monopoly Administration Date issued： 07-21-1995 Effective date: 07-21-1995 Level of Authority: Departmental Regulatory Documents Area of Law: Trade & Commerce , Foreign Economy and Trade NOTICE ON THE MANAGEMENT OF THE MONOPOLY MARKETING OF IMPORTED TOBACCO AND ITS PRODUCTS (Issued jointly by the State Economic and Trade Commission, the Ministry of Foreign Trade and Economic Cooperation, the General Administration of Customs and the State Tobacco Monopoly Bureau) The following notice on the management of the monopoly marketing of imported tobacco and its products is issued to strengthen management of imported tobacco and its products and safeguard healthy the development of the tobacco industry in accordance with the Law of the People's Republic of China on Tobacco Monopoly : 国家经济贸易委员会 海关总署 对外贸易经济合作部 国家烟草专卖局 关于加强烟草及其制品进口专卖管理的通知 依据《 中华人民共和国烟草专卖法 》，为加强对进口烟草及其制品的管理，维护烟草行业的健康发展，现就烟草及制品进口专卖管理问题通知如下： 1. Enterprises engaging in the import and export of monopolized tobacco must apply to the State Tobacco Monopoly Bureau for a special license for doing tobacco monopoly businesses. ．．．．．． 一、 凡经营烟草专卖品进出口业务的企业，必须向国家烟草专卖局申领特种烟草专卖经营企业许可证。 ．．．．．． Dear visitor,you are attempting to view a subscription-based section of lawinfochina.com. perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates specific guarantees and notification timelines regarding information accuracy and validity. The changed text weakens these guarantees by replacing 'shall guarantee' with subjective language like 'endeavor to provide... believed to be' and making the notification timeframe discretionary ('when practical'). While the provided snippets don't directly address the specific requirements of good faith, the Law Explanation highlights that Article 7 of China's Contract Law requires parties to adhere to the principle of good faith and by weakening the guarantee of information accuracy and the mandatory notification period, Party B could potentially be seen as not acting in good faith. The shift from mandatory guarantees to subjective beliefs and discretionary notifications directly contradicts the implicit obligation to provide reliable information within a reasonable timeframe, creating ambiguity and undermining Party A's ability to rely on the information provided by Party B.

perturbation.original_text: Party B shall be obliged to verify the actual use of the leased vehicles, assist and ensure that the Driver User purchases corresponding insurance according to the actual use of the leased vehicles; if the leased vehicle is used for online Ride- hailing operation, in case that the provisions of the local Laws on online Ride-hailing insurance are changed within the term of cooperation or the provisions of the local Laws on online Ride-hailing are officially implemented within the term of cooperation, Party B shall inform the Driver User within 3 working days after the change of local Laws and regulations on Ride-hailing or the effective implementation thereof, assist the Driver User and ensure that the insurance of the leased vehicle shall be changed in accordance with the provisions of local Laws on ride-hailing to make the insurance of the leased vehicle comply with the provisions of relevant Laws and regulations on Ride-hailing at that time.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B will attempt to verify the actual use of the leased vehicles and should encourage the Driver User to purchase insurance they feel is adequate according to their perception of the use of the leased vehicles; if the leased vehicle is used for online Ride- hailing operation, if local laws on online Ride-hailing insurance are modified or implemented during this cooperation, Party B will endeavor to communicate with the Driver User and assist them in considering whether to change the insurance of the leased vehicle.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall be obliged to verify" to "will attempt to verify" and "assist and ensure that the Driver User purchases corresponding insurance" to "encourage the Driver User to purchase insurance they feel is adequate," the obligation to ensure proper insurance coverage is significantly weakened. Removing the mandatory timeframe of 3 working days and the binding language surrounding legal compliance related to insurance requirements introduces ambiguity and potentially violates regulations about ensuring adequate insurance for ride-hailing vehicles.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Regulations on the Administration of Online Taxi Booking Services Article 17
perturbation.law_citation: Regulations on the Administration of Online Taxi Booking Services, Article 17
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 17 stipulates the responsibilities of platform companies in ensuring the safety and legality of online ride-hailing services. The modified text weakens the obligation of Party B to ensure that drivers have adequate insurance, creating an environment where drivers might not be properly insured, thus conflicting with the regulatory goal of protecting passengers and drivers through adequate insurance coverage.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: administrative law | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you administrative law Administrative law is the branch of law governing the creation and operation of administrative agencies . The powers granted to administrative agencies are particularly important, along with the substantive rules that such agencies make, and the legal relationships between agencies, other government bodies, and the public at large. Overview Administrative law encompasses laws and legal principles governing the administration and regulation of government agencies (both federal and state ). Agencies are delegated power by Congress (or in the case of a state agency, the state legislature ), to take on the responsibility for carrying out certain prerogatives from Congress. Agencies are created through their own organic statutes , which establish new laws, and in doing so, create the respective agencies to interpret, administer , and enforce those new laws. Generally, administrative agencies are created to protect a public interest rather than to vindicate private rights. Structure of Agencies Some textualist scholars argue that administrative agencies are unconstitutional , particularly because of the arguably unconstitutional delegation of law-making power. Nonetheless, the current jurisprudence in administrative law deems agencies constitutional, and necessary in a functional and pragmatic sense. Indeed, because Congress cannot enforce and administer every law it enacts, it must rely on the agencies to administer, enforce, and otherwise regulate various federal laws that Congress has enacted. Some of the most notable agencies are the executive agencies such as the Department of Justice and the Department of Defense . Other examples include some independent agencies such as the Federal Trade Commission , Securities and Exchange Commission , and the Environmental Protection Agency , all of which are independent from the direct control of the President. These agencies have enormous power to enact various rules defining and interpreting relevant statutes, enforce those rules, and adjudicate matters according to those regulations. Statutory Regulation of Agencies However, despite the agencies’ enormous powers, the governmental agencies must still act within Constitutional and statutory parameters. These and other limits have been codified , for the most part, into a statute known as the Administrative Procedure Act (APA) and state analogs, which provides for the roles and powers of all the agencies, and the procedures by which they must abide by in all their functions. APA categorizes administrative functions into formal and informal rulemaking and adjudication , which have binding effects, as well as guidance, which has no binding effect. The APA further prescribes certain procedural rules by which the agencies need to follow before acting in one of the functions listed. The APA is a remedial statute designed to ensure uniformity and openness in the procedures used by federal agencies. The Act is a comprehensive regulatory scheme governing regulations, adjudications, and rulemaking in general terms. The APA is the major source for federal administrative agency law, while state agencies' administration and regulation are governed by comparable state acts. For example, under the federal APA and judicial interpretations of the APA, agencies purporting to engage in the rulemaking function must give notice of proposed rulemaking before adopting the final rule. The final rule must follow a logical outgrowth of the proposed rule and must display the agencies’ consideration of all comments received and reviewed. In its adjudicatory functions, the agencies often have their own judicial body, known as the Administrative Law Judge , who are part of their respective agency, but must be independent from the agency officials involved in certain actions. The administrative law judges are to adjudicate claims or d
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens Party B's obligation to ensure drivers have adequate insurance. The original text states Party B "shall be obliged to verify" and "assist and ensure" that drivers purchase insurance, while the modified text changes this to "will attempt to verify" and "encourage the Driver User to purchase insurance they feel is adequate". This creates ambiguity about the required insurance coverage and potentially violates regulations for ride-hailing services. The Perturbation Explanation notes that removing the mandatory timeframe and binding language related to legal compliance introduces ambiguity. Scraped Law Snippet 1 mentions that administrative agencies are created to protect a public interest, and that agencies are delegated power by Congress to take on the responsibility for carrying out certain prerogatives from Congress. The obligation to ensure proper insurance coverage falls under this umbrella, implying that weakening such obligation would be contradictory to its intended purpose.

perturbation.original_text: In case that the Driver User fails to pay the monthly rent in full and on time as stipulated in the Financial Leasing Agreement, Party B shall deal with it according to the following procedures: a notification shall be sent to the Driver User to require the Driver User to pay the monthly rent within a reasonable time firstly. If the Driver User refuses to correct and delays the payment of the monthly rent for more than 30 days, Party B can take reasonable measures to recover the vehicle or terminate or waive the Financial Leasing Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In case that the Driver User fails to pay the monthly rent in full and on time as stipulated in the Financial Leasing Agreement, Party B may consider sending a notification to the Driver User to encourage payment of the monthly rent. If the Driver User does not respond positively and payment is delayed, Party B might explore measures to address the situation.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall deal with it according to the following procedures: a notification shall be sent" to "may consider sending a notification" and replacing the defined process with "might explore measures to address the situation," the original obligation for Party B to take specific actions regarding late payments is removed. This creates ambiguity as to what actions Party B is legally obligated to take, potentially contradicting laws protecting lessees from arbitrary or unfair repossession practices.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law of the People's Republic of China Article 107
perturbation.law_citation: Contract Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 107
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 107 establishes the principle that if a party fails to perform its contractual obligations or its performance does not conform to the agreement, it shall bear liability for breach of contract. The modified text provides no clarity regarding what steps will be taken in the event of a default, creating an environment where the response becomes discretionary. Such a setup may be deemed not to conform to the good faith requirements for contract enforcement.

perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates specific actions (sending a notification and following procedures) if the Driver User fails to pay. The changed text replaces 'shall deal with it according to the following procedures: a notification shall be sent' with discretionary language 'may consider sending a notification' and replaces the defined process with 'might explore measures to address the situation.' This weakens the obligation and creates ambiguity, which, as the Perturbation Explanation states, potentially contradicts laws protecting lessees from arbitrary or unfair repossession practices. The Law Explanation states that failure to perform contractual obligations leads to liability for breach of contract, and the change could be deemed not to conform to the good faith requirements for contract enforcement due to the ambiguity it introduces. While the Scraped Law Snippets aren't directly relevant, the weakening of the obligation from 'shall' to 'may' and the overall ambiguity introduced support the assessment of a contradiction.
